question: Based on Figure 5.4-1, how does the helical coil SG arrangement differ visually from a conventional U-tube SG?

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and form part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself serving as the steam generator shell and outer pressure boundary. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the necessary pressure boundaries.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the applicable material specifications for components such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references other sections for further design specifics of key components.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the Reactor Coolant System's high-point vents and associated components, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for safety and operability. It details the classification, testing frequency, and procedures for DHRS actuation valves, including full-stroke exercising during cold shutdown conditions and initial in-situ testing for the first reactor module to verify heat removal capability.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and all copies must retain the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, detailing its structure and topics.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and core bypass, ensuring low pressure losses to support natural circulation, and confirms that certain uncertainties, like SG tube plugging, do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located within the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. It interfaces with several other systems, including chemical control, emergency cooling, and control rod drives.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components are conducted according to the latest applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section describes the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS), which uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to ensure safe and efficient cooling, maintain proper water chemistry, and uniformly distribute soluble boron under all operating conditions, thereby protecting fuel integrity during normal and abnormal events.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4.
